internet|apps.question: Search engines build indexes of what?
internet|apps.explanation: Search engines index web pages so users can retrieve them later.

internet|apps.question: Browser cookies are mainly used for what?
internet|apps.explanation: Cookies store small bits of data such as sessions and preferences.

internet|apps.question: An API lets one program request what from another?
internet|apps.explanation: APIs expose software services and data to other programs.

internet|apps.question: Dropbox is primarily used for what?
internet|apps.explanation: Dropbox centers on storing, syncing, and sharing files online.

internet|apps.question: In Slack, work is organized into dedicated spaces called what?
internet|apps.explanation: Slack says work is organized into dedicated spaces called channels.

internet|gaming.question: Streaming gameplay involves broadcasting video over what?
MMORPGs to many players inhabiting what?
Lag in online games stems mainly from network what?
Loot boxes with randomized what purchases?
Voice chat in games to real-time what transmission?
Speedrunning as completing games emphasizing what metric?
Cross-platform play allows different what to play together?
Cloud gaming to running games on remote what?
internet|gaming.explanation: Cloud gaming executes on remote servers.

internet|memes.question: Who introduced the term "meme" in 1976?
internet|memes.explanation: Richard Dawkins introduced the term meme in 1976.

internet|memes.question: The word "meme" comes from a Greek term meaning what?
internet|memes.explanation: The word meme comes from a Greek term meaning imitated.

internet|memes.question: Internet memes usually spread by what process?
internet|memes.explanation: Internet memes usually spread by imitation.

internet|memes.question: A GIF is best known for what kind of image behavior?
internet|memes.explanation: GIFs are widely used for short looping animations online.

internet|memes.question: Emoji are standardized what kind of symbols?
internet|memes.explanation: Emoji are standardized pictographs used in digital communication.

internet|memes.question: Hashtags are mainly used to group posts under shared what?
internet|memes.explanation: Hashtags help organize posts around shared topics and keywords.

internet|memes.question: The name "copypasta" comes from doing what to text repeatedly?
internet|memes.explanation: Copypasta refers to blocks of text repeatedly copied and pasted online.

internet|memes.question: Reaction images are usually posted to convey what?
internet|memes.explanation: Reaction images are used to express emotion or tone in online conversations.

internet|memes.question: Which cryptocurrency was created as a joke based on a popular meme?
internet|memes.explanation: Dogecoin was created as a joke based on a popular meme.

internet|memes.question: Internet memes often take the form of pictures, videos, or what other broad category?
internet|memes.explanation: Internet memes often appear as pictures, videos, or other media shared online.

internet|social-media.question: LinkedIn describes itself as the world's largest what?
internet|social-media.explanation: LinkedIn calls itself the world's largest professional network.

internet|social-media.question: On what date did LinkedIn officially launch?
internet|social-media.explanation: LinkedIn says it was officially launched on May 5, 2003.

internet|social-media.question: TikTok says it is a destination for what kind of videos?
internet|social-media.explanation: TikTok describes itself as a destination for short-form mobile videos.

internet|social-media.question: TikTok says it captures and presents moments that matter directly from what?
internet|social-media.explanation: TikTok's mission statement says it presents creativity and moments directly from the mobile phone.

internet|social-media.question: On Reddit, topic-based smaller communities are called what?
internet|social-media.explanation: Reddit's official help pages call its smaller communities subreddits.

internet|social-media.question: Reddit says subreddits are created and moderated by whom?
internet|social-media.explanation: Reddit says its communities are created and moderated by redditors.

internet|social-media.question: Reddit communities can be public, restricted, private, and what premium type?
internet|social-media.explanation: Reddit lists premium-only as one of its community types.

internet|social-media.question: Pinterest uses what to save and organize Pins?
internet|social-media.explanation: Pinterest says boards are the place to save and organize ideas.

internet|social-media.question: A Pinterest group board is a board with what?
internet|social-media.explanation: Pinterest says group boards are boards with collaborators.

internet|social-media.question: Pinterest boards can be public or what hidden type?
internet|social-media.explanation: Pinterest says secret boards are visible only to you and invited collaborators.

internet|tech.question: In an HTML document, which element contains the visible page content?
internet|tech.explanation: The body element contains the page content users actually see.

internet|tech.question: Which TCP port is the default for HTTP when no port is specified?
internet|tech.explanation: RFC 7230 uses port 80 as the default for HTTP URIs without an explicit port.

internet|tech.question: TCP is usually described as operating at which layer?
internet|tech.explanation: TCP is the transport-layer protocol responsible for reliable ordered delivery.

internet|tech.question: What does an IP address identify on a network?
internet|tech.explanation: An IP address identifies a host or device on a network.

internet|tech.question: HTTPS is HTTP plus what added protection?
internet|tech.explanation: HTTPS secures HTTP traffic by using TLS encryption.

internet|tech.question: Wi-Fi is mainly used to provide what kind of access?
internet|tech.explanation: Wi-Fi uses radio waves to provide wireless local-area network access.

internet|tech.question: Bluetooth is designed for short-range wireless connections between what?
internet|tech.explanation: Bluetooth is meant for short-range links between nearby personal devices.

internet|tech.question: Fiber-optic cables transmit data using what?
internet|tech.explanation: Fiber-optic systems carry data as pulses of light.

internet|tech.question: Routers are used to forward packets between what?
internet|tech.explanation: Routers direct packets from one network to another.

internet|tech.question: Network latency is commonly measured in what units?
internet|tech.explanation: Latency is a measure of delay, commonly reported in milliseconds.

internet|websites.question: The early World Wide Web was built around linking documents with what?
internet|websites.explanation: Tim Berners-Lee's early World Wide Web project was built around hypertext links between documents.

internet|websites.question: Which standards body publishes HTML recommendations?
internet|websites.explanation: HTML recommendations are published by the World Wide Web Consortium, or W3C.

internet|websites.question: Which organization publishes RFC documents such as RFC 7230?
internet|websites.explanation: RFC documents are published through the RFC Editor.

internet|websites.question: Wikipedia is best described as what kind of site?
internet|websites.explanation: Wikipedia is an online encyclopedia edited by volunteers.

internet|websites.question: What do e-commerce sites primarily sell online?
internet|websites.explanation: E-commerce sites are built to sell goods or services online.

internet|websites.question: Blogs are known for publishing dated what?
internet|websites.explanation: Blogs typically publish dated posts or articles.

internet|websites.question: Internet forums are centered on threaded what?
internet|websites.explanation: Forums organize conversations as threaded discussions.

internet|websites.question: A landing page is usually built for a specific marketing what?
internet|websites.explanation: Landing pages are usually designed around a specific marketing campaign.

internet|websites.question: A CDN improves website speed by moving content closer to whom?
internet|websites.explanation: Content delivery networks speed up sites by caching content closer to end users.

internet|websites.question: A paywall blocks access until a user does what?
internet|websites.explanation: A paywall restricts access until payment or subscription is provided.

animals|birds.question: What is the largest living bird?
animals|birds.explanation: The ostrich is the largest living bird.

animals|birds.question: Which bird can fly backward?
animals|birds.explanation: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can truly fly backward.

animals|birds.question: Penguins are native to which hemisphere?
animals|birds.explanation: Penguins are native to the Southern Hemisphere.

animals|birds.question: Which birds of prey are famous for forward-facing eyes and silent flight?
animals|birds.explanation: Owls are known for forward-facing eyes and quiet flight.

animals|birds.question: Which birds are especially known for mimicking human speech?
animals|birds.explanation: Parrots are especially well known for vocal mimicry.

animals|birds.question: What is the national bird of the United States?
animals|birds.explanation: The bald eagle is the national bird of the United States.

animals|birds.question: Which bird is famous for the fastest hunting dive?
animals|birds.explanation: The peregrine falcon is famous for its extremely fast dive.

animals|birds.question: Which flightless bird is native to New Zealand?
animals|birds.explanation: The kiwi is a flightless bird native to New Zealand.

animals|birds.question: Which bird gets its pink color largely from pigments in its diet?
animals|birds.explanation: Flamingos get much of their pink color from pigments in their food.

animals|birds.question: Emperor penguins breed during which Antarctic season?
animals|birds.explanation: Emperor penguins breed during the Antarctic winter.

animals|dinosaurs.question: Modern birds are generally accepted as descendants of what larger group?
animals|dinosaurs.explanation: Modern birds are generally accepted as descendants of dinosaurs.

animals|dinosaurs.question: Stegosaurus is famous for the large plates along which part of its body?
animals|dinosaurs.explanation: Stegosaurus is known for the large plates along its back.

animals|dinosaurs.question: How many facial horns did Triceratops have?
animals|dinosaurs.explanation: Triceratops had three prominent facial horns.

animals|dinosaurs.question: Velociraptor lived during which geologic period?
animals|dinosaurs.explanation: Velociraptor lived during the Cretaceous Period.

animals|dinosaurs.question: Which flying reptiles were close relatives of dinosaurs but not dinosaurs themselves?
animals|dinosaurs.explanation: Pterosaurs were flying reptiles related to dinosaurs, not true dinosaurs.

animals|dinosaurs.question: Long-necked plant-eaters like Diplodocus belong to what dinosaur group?
animals|dinosaurs.explanation: Diplodocus was a sauropod, the long-necked dinosaur group.

animals|dinosaurs.question: Which period came right before the Jurassic?
animals|dinosaurs.explanation: The Triassic Period came before the Jurassic.

animals|dinosaurs.question: Ankylosaurus is famous for having what at the end of its tail?
animals|dinosaurs.explanation: Ankylosaurus is known for the club at the end of its tail.

animals|dinosaurs.question: Tyrannosaurus is usually classified as what type of eater?
animals|dinosaurs.explanation: Tyrannosaurus is usually classified as a carnivorous dinosaur.

animals|dinosaurs.question: Archaeopteryx is often described as an early link between dinosaurs and what modern animals?
animals|dinosaurs.explanation: Archaeopteryx is often described as linking dinosaurs and birds.

animals|ocean.question: What is the largest animal on Earth?
animals|ocean.explanation: The blue whale is the largest animal on Earth.

animals|ocean.question: How many arms does an octopus have?
Coral reefs are built by colonies of what tiny animals?

animals|ocean.question: Dolphins breathe air through what opening on top of the head?

animals|ocean.question: What ocean reptile comes ashore to lay its eggs?

animals|ocean.question: Which ocean animal is well known for squirting ink as a defense?

brands|companies.question: The LEGO Group began in which country?

brands|companies.question: Nintendo was founded in which Japanese city?

brands|companies.explanation: Instagram is part of Meta Platforms.

brands|companies.question: LinkedIn is part of which company?

brands|companies.explanation: Amazon announced its acquisition of Whole Foods Market in 2017.

brands|companies.question: Spotify was founded in which country?
Samsung was founded in which country?
brands|companies.explanation: Samsung was founded in South Korea.

brands|companies.question: Google began as a project at which university?
brands|companies.explanation: Google began as a project at Stanford University.

movies-tv|awards.question: What is the official name of the prize nicknamed the Oscar?
movies-tv|awards.explanation: The Oscar is officially the Academy Award of Merit.

movies-tv|awards.question: Which film won Best Picture at the first Academy Awards?
movies-tv|awards.explanation: Wings won Best Picture at the first Academy Awards.

movies-tv|awards.question: Which film won Best Picture at the 70th Academy Awards?
movies-tv|awards.explanation: Titanic won Best Picture at the 70th Academy Awards.

movies-tv|awards.question: Which film became the first non-English-language winner of Best Picture?
movies-tv|awards.explanation: Parasite became the first non-English-language Best Picture winner.

movies-tv|awards.question: The Emmy Awards honor excellence in what medium?
movies-tv|awards.explanation: The Emmy Awards recognize excellence in television.

movies-tv|awards.question: The Golden Globes recognize achievement in film and what other medium?
movies-tv|awards.explanation: The Golden Globes recognize achievement in both film and television.

movies-tv|awards.question: The Tony Awards honor achievement in what field?
movies-tv|awards.explanation: The Tony Awards honor achievement in Broadway theater.

movies-tv|awards.question: The Oscar statuette shows a knight holding what?
movies-tv|awards.explanation: The Oscar statuette depicts a knight holding a sword.

movies-tv|awards.question: In what year were the first Academy Awards held?
movies-tv|awards.explanation: The first Academy Awards were held in 1929.

movies-tv|awards.question: During World War II, Oscar statuettes were temporarily made of what instead of metal?
movies-tv|awards.explanation: During World War II, Oscar statuettes were made of plaster because of metal shortages.

movies-tv|movies.question: In Finding Nemo, what kind of fish is Nemo?
In Back to the Future, Doc Brown turns what car into a time machine?
movies-tv|movies.explanation: Doc Brown builds the time machine from a DeLorean.

movies-tv|movies.question: Han Solo pilots what famous ship?
movies-tv|movies.explanation: Han Solo is the pilot of the Millennium Falcon.

movies-tv|movies.question: In Toy Story, Buzz Lightyear believes he is what?
movies-tv|movies.explanation: Buzz Lightyear believes he is a real space ranger.

movies-tv|movies.question: What kind of animal is Simba in The Lion King?

movies-tv|movies.explanation: Titanic is set aboard the RMS Titanic.

movies-tv|movies.question: In Ratatouille, Remy is what animal?
In Harry Potter and the Sorcerers Stone, students attend what school?
movies-tv|movies.explanation: Harry attends Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry.

movies-tv|movies.question: What is the name of the kingdom ruled by Elsa and Anna in Frozen?
movies-tv|movies.explanation: Elsa and Anna rule the kingdom of Arendelle.

movies-tv|movies.question: In Jaws, what animal terrorizes Amity Island?
movies-tv|movies.explanation: Jaws centers on a great white shark attacking Amity Island.

pop-culture|internet.question: What does GIF stand for?
pop-culture|internet.explanation: GIF stands for Graphics Interchange Format.

pop-culture|internet.explanation: URL stands for Uniform Resource Locator.

pop-culture|internet.explanation: Posts on Twitter were originally called tweets.

pop-culture|internet.explanation: HTTP 404 means the page was not found.

pop-culture|internet.explanation: CAPTCHA is designed to distinguish humans from bots.

pop-culture|internet.explanation: A podcast is usually an episodic audio program distributed online.

pop-culture|internet.question: What does WWW stand for?
pop-culture|internet.explanation: WWW stands for World Wide Web.

pop-culture|internet.question: Twitch is best known for what kind of online content?
pop-culture|internet.explanation: Twitch is best known for livestreamed content.

pop-culture|memes.question: The Doge meme is based on what breed of dog?

pop-culture|memes.explanation: Success Kid shows a toddler clenching sand at the beach.

pop-culture|memes.explanation: Pepe the Frog is a frog character.

pop-culture|memes.explanation: Nyan Cat is depicted with a Pop-Tart body.

pop-culture|viral.explanation: The Harlem Shake trend involved sudden group dancing.
